Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \cos^{-1}\left(\frac{u}{5}\right).

\text{Using the reference triangle, we define the sides based on the inverse function:}

x = u, \quad y = \sqrt{-u^{2} + 25}, \quad r = 5

\text{From this, we write the required trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{-u^{2} + 25}}{5}

\cos(\alpha) = \frac{u}{5}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = 2\cos^2(\alpha) - 1

\cos(2\alpha) = 2\left(\frac{u}{5}\right)^2 - 1

\text{Simplify the algebraic expression:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{2 \, u^{2} - 25}{25}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \sec^{-1}\left(\frac{24}{5u}\right).

\text{Using the reference triangle, we define the sides based on the inverse function:}

x = 5u, \quad y = \sqrt{-25 \, u^{2} + 576}, \quad r = 24

\text{From this, we write the required trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{-25 \, u^{2} + 576}}{24}

\cos(\alpha) = \frac{5u}{24}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(\frac{\sqrt{-25 \, u^{2} + 576}}{24}\right)\left(\frac{5u}{24}\right)

\text{Simplify the algebraic expression:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{-25 \, u^{2} + 576} u}{288}

Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \sin^{-1}\left(3u\right).

\text{Using the reference triangle, we define the sides based on the inverse function:}

x = \sqrt{-9 \, u^{2} + 1}, \quad y = 3u, \quad r = 1

\text{From this, we write the required trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = 3u

\cos(\alpha) = \sqrt{-9 \, u^{2} + 1}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = 1 - 2\sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = 1 - 2\left(3u\right)^2

\text{Simplify the algebraic expression:}

\cos(2\alpha) = -18 \, u^{2} + 1

\text{Using the reference triangle, we define the sides based on the inverse function:}

x = 4 \, \sqrt{u^{2} - 25}, \quad y = 20, \quad r = 4u

\text{From this, we write the required trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{20}{4u}

\cos(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{u^{2} - 25}}{4u}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \cos(2\alpha) = 1 - 2\sin^2(\alpha)

\cos(2\alpha) = 1 - 2\left(\frac{20}{4u}\right)^2

\text{Simplify the algebraic expression:}

\cos(2\alpha) = \frac{u^{2} - 50}{u^{2}}

x = \sqrt{-u^{2} + 1}, \quad y = u, \quad r = 1

\text{From this, we write the required trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = u

\cos(\alpha) = \sqrt{-u^{2} + 1}

\text{Apply the double-angle identity:}

\sin(2\alpha) = \sin(2\alpha) = 2\sin(\alpha)\cos(\alpha)

\sin(2\alpha) = 2\left(u\right)\left(\sqrt{-u^{2} + 1}\right)

\text{Simplify the algebraic expression:}

\sin(2\alpha) = 2 \, \sqrt{-u^{2} + 1} u
